title: Figure 6 from: Pourmoghaddam MJ, Lambert C, Voglmayr H, Khodaparast SA, Stadler M (2026) A polyphasic study of Hypoxylaceae (Xylariales, Ascomycota) in Iran: new species and records from Hyrcanian forests. MycoKeys 131: 1-40. https://doi.org/10.3897/mycokeys.131.186541
author: Pourmoghaddam, Mohammad Javad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 6 Hypoxylon crocopeplum (GUM 1611). A. Stromatal habit; B. Close-up view of stromatal surface, with stromatal pigments in 10% KOH; C. Section of stroma showing conspicuous perithecial mounds; D. Stroma in horizontal section showing perithecia, granules beneath the surface and between perithecia and ostioles; E. Immature and mature asci in 10% KOH; F. Ascus in water; G. Immature ascus tip in Melzer's reagent; H. Mature ascus tip in Melzer's reagent; I. Ascospores in 10% KOH with straight to slightly sigmoid germ slit spore-length on convex side; J. Ascospores in 10% KOH with dehiscent perispore; K. Detail of striated perispore. Scale bars: 5 mm (A); 2 mm (B); 0.5 mm (C); 0.25 mm (D); 20 µm (E, F); 10 µm (G–K).
